results.title: Norwegian contrasts
results.brief_description: Tourists come to admire the physical and cultural beauties of Norway, but there is more to the country than that…there has always been the sea. Norwegian fishermen are responsible for 5 percent of the global catch. The film goes on to cover the problems associated with agriculture, the drift to the cities, industrialisation and pollution.

results.title: Northern New England, USA: an invitation
results.brief_description: Northern New England is a place where, each year nature becomes the artist. This does not only apply to autumn, when nature splashes the mountains with every colour of the rainbow, but to the rest of the seasons as well, making Northern New England an all year round vacation paradise.

results.title: North west link
results.brief_description: Made by Telecom and the Postmaster-Generals dept. the film shows the work of laying a 600-mile coaxial cable from Perth to Carnarvon, W.A. The pipe is a link in the national broad-band telecommunication network extending the range of subscriber trunk dialling and television relay facilities.

results.title: North-West Coastal Highway
results.brief_description: Shows how the Main Roads Department of Western Australia is completing a modern road from Carnarvon to Port Hedland as part of the National Highway number 1 encircling the continent. Explains some aspects of its effect on the development and prosperity of the area.

results.title: Normal and abnormal neurologic function in infancy
results.creator_credit: Dept of Pediatrics, Ohio State University
results.brief_description: This film is designed to illustrate how infant motor function can provide a valuable diagnostic tool for early detection of intellectual impairment. Employing extensive clinical footage, the filmmakers have sought to depict characteristic defects of co-ordination where these exist either independently of, or as a result of, childhood brain injury.

results.title: Noguchi, a sculptor's world
results.brief_description: A film biography of Isamu Noguchi, a foremost modern twentieth century artist, whom Buckminster Fuller called “The comprehensive artist without peer in our time”. Speaking directly to his audience and narrating the film, Noguchi lends insight into his thinking, philosophy and versatile creative life.

results.title: Nobody told me
results.brief_description: So that “nobody told me” is no longer an excuse, the film carefully explains how floods occur and, also, the work of the Grand River Conservation Authority in Ontario, Canada, to police the flood plain regulation. The regulation itself is explained in great detail.

results.title: No fixed address
results.brief_description: Follows a television reporter who becomes for a day and a night one of Melbourne’s homeless men. Shows the queue for lodging and food at the Gill Memorial Home, the City Mission, and other hostels and refuges. The reporter talks to some of these men, most of whom are unemployed - many unemployable in their present state. They tell of their daytime wandering of the streets, their difficulty in finding employment and the escape for some that alcohol brings. The problem of rehabilitation is discussed.

results.title: No fences for Baronne: Baronne Champs libres
results.creator_credit: Claude R Camredon and Jacscam Enterprises
results.brief_description: This film describes the contrast of freedom and work for the horses in the beautiful and wild region of the Swiss Jura. It also presents exciting, unique events and popular races.
